---
title: "Applying cell weights"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Libraries and set up

```{r message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
.libPaths("/enna/nobackup/biostat/Rpackages/v4/")
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(spicyR)
  library(SpatialDatasets)
  library(tidyverse)
  library(tidySingleCellExperiment)
  library(scClassify)
  library(caret) 
  library(spatstat.geom)
  library(data.table)
  library(Statial)
  library(BiocParallel)
  library(lme4)
  library(lmerTest)
})
```

```{r message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
spe = SpatialDatasets::spe_Amancherla_2025()
```

## EDA

```{r}
# spe$patient_id |> unique() # unique patient id
# spe$Sample |> unique() # sample id 
# spe$patient_biopsy |> unique() # sample (biopsy) id with info on whether biopsy is pre or post transplant

# spe$patient_cellular_grading |> unique() # cellular rejection grade (patient level)
# spe$patient_antibody_grading |> unique() # antibody rejection grade (patient level)
```


```{r}
colData(spe) |> as.data.frame() |> distinct(patient_id, sample_id, patient_rejection_type, biopsy_rejection_type) |>
  arrange(patient_id)
```

```{r}
spe$biopsy_timing |> unique()
```

```{r}
spe$cav_grade |> unique()
```

```{r}
hist(assay(spe, "counts") |> as.vector(), breaks = 100, main = "Histogram of counts", xlab = "Counts")
```


```{r}
hist(assay(spe, "logcounts") |> as.vector(), breaks = 100, main = "Histogram of logcounts", xlab = "Logcounts")
```

```{r}
# likely went through Seurat's ScaleData pipeline = x-mean/sd, hard capped at 10
hist(assay(spe, "scaledata") |> as.vector(), breaks = 100, main = "Histogram of scaled data", xlab = "Scaled data")
```

```{r}
max(assay(spe, "scaledata"))  

zeroFrac = mean(assay(spe, "counts") == 0)
zeroFrac  # probably very high, e.g. >80%
```

```{r}
spe$ct_second_pass = as.character(spe$ct_second_pass)
spe$ct_second_pass |> unique()
```

```{r}
# whether rejection responded to immunotherapy
spe$resolution_broad |> unique()
```

```{r}
# set r = 100 based on the size of these images (50 might be too small?)
spe |> colData() |> as.data.frame() |>
  dplyr::group_by(Sample) |>                                                                                                                           
  dplyr::summarise(max_x = max(adj_x_centroid), max_y = max(adj_y_centroid)) 
```


## Subset data

```{r}
set.seed(51773)

# filter to pre transplant
# randomly pick 10 patients from each group 
patient_idx = spe |>
  filter(biopsy_timing == "post" & patient_rejection_type == "cellular_rejection") |>
  group_by(resolution_broad) |>
  filter(patient_id %in% sample(unique(patient_id), min(10, n_distinct(patient_id)))) |>
  ungroup() |> pull(patient_id) |> unique()

speSub = spe[, colData(spe)$patient_id %in% patient_idx, ]
```

```{r}
speSub$patient_id = droplevels(speSub$patient_id)
speSub$Sample = droplevels(speSub$Sample)

# rename columns to make it easy
speSub = speSub |> rename(c("x" = "adj_x_centroid", "y" = "adj_y_centroid", "cellType" = "ct_second_pass",
                            "imageID" = "Sample", "subject" = "patient_id", "cellID" = "X"))
```

## Generate closePairs 

```{r}
# extract cell data
cells = colData(speSub) |> as.data.frame()

# split by image/sample ID
imageList = split(cells, cells$imageID)

# set radius
r = 100

imageResults = lapply(imageList, function(image) {
    image$cellID = factor(seq_len(nrow(image)))
    ow = Statial::makeWindow(image, window = "convex")
    
    # generate closePairs object
    imagePPP = spatstat.geom::ppp(x = image$x,
      y = image$y,
      window = ow,
      marks = data.frame(cellType = image$cellType, cellID = image$cellID))                                                                                        
    # returns a data frame where each row is a pair of cells within radius r
    # columns = i, j, d (distance between them)
    closePairs = spatstat.geom::closepairs(imagePPP, r, what = "ijd", distinct = FALSE) |> data.frame()
    
    # extract cell type vector
    cellTypes = imagePPP$marks$cellType                                                                                                                           
    # add cell ID
    names(cellTypes) = imagePPP$marks$cellID
    
    # for each pair, look up the cell type of the "i" cell
    closePairs$cellTypeI = cellTypes[closePairs$i]                                                                                                                 
    # look up the cell type of the "j" cell
    closePairs$cellTypeJ = cellTypes[closePairs$j]
    
    # convert i from plain integer index to factor (used later - i is an idex when performing edge correction)
    closePairs$i = factor(closePairs$i, levels = imagePPP$marks$cellID)
    closePairs = as.data.table(closePairs)
    
    # edge correction
    # compute correction weight for each cell (between 0 and 1)
    # represents the proportion of the search circle (radius r) that falls inside the tissue window
    # cells well inside the tissue get 1, cells near the edge get lower weights 
    edge = Statial:::.borderEdge(imagePPP, r)                                                                                                                  
    
    # invert the weights (increase contribution of cells near the edge)
    edge = data.frame(i = factor(imagePPP$marks$cellID, levels = imagePPP$marks$cellID),
                      edge = 1 / edge)
    
    # add correction weights to closePairs 
    closePairs = dplyr::left_join(closePairs, edge[, c("i", "edge")], by = "i")
    
    list(closePairs = closePairs,
      area = spatstat.geom::area(imagePPP))
})

```

## Calculate L-function (unweighted)

```{r}
# get all cell type pairs
cellTypes = unique(cells$cellType)
pairs = expand.grid(child1 = cellTypes, child2 = cellTypes, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

Lfunc = bplapply(names(imageResults), function(imgID) {
  closePairs = imageResults[[imgID]]$closePairs
    area = imageResults[[imgID]]$area
    
    counts = closePairs[, .(n = sum(edge)), by = .(i, cellTypeI, cellTypeJ)]
    counts = dcast(counts, i + cellTypeI ~ cellTypeJ, value.var = "n", fill = 0)
    
    # loop over every cell type pair in image - and calc L-function
    pairResults = lapply(seq_len(nrow(pairs)), function(k) {
      child1 = pairs$child1[k]
      child2 = pairs$child2[k]
      
      # if a cell type is absent in the image return NA
      if (!child1 %in% closePairs$cellTypeI | !child2 %in% closePairs$cellTypeJ) {
        return(data.frame(imageID = imgID, child1 = child1, child2 = child2, L = NA))
      }
      
      # calculate L function and return one-row df
      L = Statial:::.Lfunction(closePairs, counts, child1, child2, r, area)
      data.frame(imageID = imgID, child1 = child1, child2 = child2, L = L)
    })                                                                                                                                                             
    dplyr::bind_rows(pairResults)                                                                                                                                                                                   
}, BPPARAM = MulticoreParam(workers = 20))

Lfunc = dplyr::bind_rows(Lfunc)   
```

```{r}
# combine L-function metrics with metadata
cDat = colData(speSub) |> as.data.frame() |> dplyr::distinct(subject, imageID, slide, biopsy_cellular_grading, biopsy_antibody_grading,
                                                             biopsy_rejection_type, days_from_transplant_to_biopsy, biopsy_timing, age_at_biopsy,
                                                             cav_grade, death, patient_cellular_grading, patient_rejection_type, resolution_broad,
                                                             treatment)

Lfunc = merge(Lfunc, cDat, by = "imageID")
Lfunc$cellType_pair = paste0(Lfunc$child1, "__", Lfunc$child2)

head(Lfunc)
```

### Mixed-effects models

```{r warning = F, message = F}
# mixed-effects models for each cell type pair
pairList = dplyr::distinct(Lfunc, child1, child2)

noWeightsModels = lapply(seq_len(nrow(pairList)), function(k) {
  child1 = pairList$child1[k]
  child2 = pairList$child2[k]
  
  dat = Lfunc |> 
    dplyr::filter(child1 == !!child1, child2 == !!child2) |>
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(L), !is.na(resolution_broad))
  
  # need at least 2 groups and enough observations
  if (length(unique(dat$resolution_broad)) < 2 | nrow(dat) < 5) {
    return(data.frame(child1 = child1, child2 = child2, p.value = NA, estimate = NA))
  }
   
  fit = tryCatch(lmer(L ~ resolution_broad + (1 | subject), data = dat),
    error = function(e) NULL)
  
  if (is.null(fit)) {
    return(data.frame(child1 = child1, child2 = child2, p.value = NA, estimate = NA))
  }
  
  coef = summary(fit)$coefficients 
  
  # second row is the resolution_broad effect
  data.frame(child1 = child1, child2 = child2, estimate = coef[2, "Estimate"], p.value = coef[2, "Pr(>|t|)"])                                                                                                                                                                                                               
})

noWeightsModels = dplyr::bind_rows(noWeightsModels)
noWeightsModels = noWeightsModels |> rename(c(from = child1, to = child2))

# multiple testing correction
noWeightsModels$p.adj = p.adjust(noWeightsModels$p.value, method = "BH")
```
